subject: Public Health
title: Contact Tracing
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Contact Tracing.

hook.text: Find one infected person, trace their contacts, test and isolate—contact tracing has stopped outbreaks for over.

buildup.text: Contact tracers interview cases, identify close contacts, and monitor them for symptoms or positive tests.

discovery.text: During the Ebola outbreak in West Africa, contact tracing identified thousands of contacts—breaking transmission chains.

twist.text: COVID-19 overwhelmed contact tracing systems worldwide—too many cases, too few tracers, too much community spread.

climax.text: Digital contact tracing apps emerged during COVID—but privacy concerns limited adoption in many countries.

punchline.text: Tracing contacts is simple in theory—scaling it during a pandemic is the real.

quiz.question: What is the goal of contact tracing?
options: To identify and isolate contacts to break transmission chains
To develop new treatments for diseases
